content.claim: Poland's 2016–2017 structural reorganization reversed the 1999 reform direction by replacing 6-year elementary schools with 8-year primary schools, abolishing lower secondary schools (gymnasiums), and replacing 3-year lower secondary schools with 4-year secondary schools
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Limited to Polish context; direct applicability to Hungarian system design depends on structural similarities not yet established
Does not specify implementation timeline or transition costs in detail
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
